title: Error:/usr/bin/skopeo: 3: Syntax error: word unexpected (expecting ")"), when run skopeo copy in arm arch builder
body:
we use skopoe command to copy docker image from local archive to gcr repository
below is the sample of the command
```
skopeo --debug copy --all docker-archive:/containers/abusedecision.tar docker://gcr.io/machamp-prod/abusedecision:807cc4de-7a51-4b57-9235-67ea86819089]
```

when update the skopeo to 1.4.1
verison v1.4.1 https://github.com/containers/skopeo/commit/130f32f047b7bf9b1fb4f95c4c5b1da6255e4829

we start to see this error in arm arch builder in aws as below, we also run this in the amd host in gcp, which does not have this issue
```
/usr/bin/skopeo: 3: Syntax error: word unexpected (expecting ")") 
Exit value: 2 
```

since this is only happens on arm arch builder, also based on some online search, I suspect this is binary arch issue

how I build the binary for aws is, I create the binary by running "make bin/skopeo" in arm builder
I suppose the binary generated in arm host should be compatible with arm, but this is assumption

want to get some help on this issue
would "make bin/skopeo" create the correct arch binary? or I have to run "make local-cross", and copy correct version myself?

comments.body: Thanks for your report. `Syntax error: word unexpected` is not a Skopeo message, see e.g. https://stackoverflow.com/questions/36725864/syntax-error-word-unexpected-expecting . This looks like a bug in a script that invokes Skopeo.

this is our run log, I think this error is what skopeo binary throwed
```
2021-10-01T15:13:33.879961094Z INFO   [15:13:33.879] [pool-3-thread-1] [] c.s.m.s.c.Commands -  Running: [skopeo, --debug, copy, --all, docker-archive:/containers/abusedecision.tar, docker://gcr.io/machamp-prod/abusedecision:622d67d5-d7d9-4efc-88ae-9e1a977c83ae] 
2021-10-01T15:13:33.881264167Z WARN   [15:13:33.881] [pool-3-thread-1] [] c.s.m.s.c.Commands -  /usr/bin/skopeo: 3: Syntax error: word unexpected (expecting ")") 
2021-10-01T15:13:33.881720301Z INFO   [15:13:33.881] [pool-3-thread-1] [] c.s.m.s.c.Commands -  Exit value: 2 
```

please advise for this

\*shrug\* Does that work when run manually?

(In general, `make bin/skopeo` does generate a binary for the current local architecture; immediately afterwards `bin/skopeo --version` should work. I guess it’s possible that this is caused an architecture mismatch, reported in a confusing way by a shell, but that error message is simply not one that Skopeo itself can, to my knowledge, generate. Perhaps confirm with `strace`.)

*shrug* Does that work when run manually? <-- how do I confirm this?

some input, after genrate skopeo from arm builder, I can run skopeo -version with no issue
we are using --debug option, is there a strace option in skopeo, sorry did not find it

I wonder if I using local-cross and copy the specific arch skopeo would help in this case?

some input when we are using older (much older) version, 0.1.41 there was no issue
when I bump up to skopeo version 1.4.1 commit: 130f32f047b7bf9b1fb4f95c4c5b1da6255e4829, I start to see this error

some more update, I have tried to run the local-cross build and generate bunch of binaries 
```
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 26M Oct  2 03:15 skopeo.darwin.amd64
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 23M Oct  2 03:15 skopeo.linux.arm
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 25M Oct  2 03:16 skopeo.linux.arm64
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 25M Oct  2 03:16 skopeo.windows.386.exe
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 28M Oct  2 03:16 skopeo.windows.amd64.exe
```

however when I run the binary on the x86_64 machine (my mac)
I can not run amd64 but able to run arm binary, is it suppose to be opposite?

```
root@14d9413fd9be:/go/src/github.com/containers/skopeo/bin# uname -m
x86_64
root@14d9413fd9be:/go/src/github.com/containers/skopeo/bin# ./skopeo.darwin.amd64 --version
bash: ./skopeo.darwin.amd64: cannot execute binary file: Exec format error
root@14d9413fd9be:/go/src/github.com/containers/skopeo/bin# ./skopeo.linux.arm --version
skopeo version 1.4.1 commit: 130f32f047b7bf9b1fb4f95c4c5b1da6255e4829
root@14d9413fd9be:/go/src/github.com/containers/skopeo/bin# ./skopeo.linux.arm64 --version
skopeo version 1.4.1 commit: 130f32f047b7bf9b1fb4f95c4c5b1da6255e4829
```
@mtrmac can I get some advise? thank you

I don't have much experience with skopeo, so this is more of a general advice:

> [15:13:33.881] [pool-3-thread-1] [] c.s.m.s.c.Commands -  /usr/bin/skopeo: 3: Syntax error: word unexpected (expecting ")") 

Going by that log message, it appears to be me that you are perhaps launching this skopeo command from some Java code using Java's `ProcessBuilder` API perhaps. Have you verified that the binary it is picking up - the `/usr/bin/skopeo` is indeed the one that was built for that particular host machine architecture?

I can definitely verify that, we have only one skopeo copy in the docker container, so unlikely it is pointing to a wrong one
for above questions, do you have any advise? or maybe I should ask those in different place (since this ticket is already been closed)

Sorry I don't have much to add, since I don't have a way to reproduce this. But just another general advice, you note that this is running inside a container. Perhaps the container inside which this skopeo command is running is incompatible with the architecture of this binary? Again, these are just wild guesses. You probably will have to strip out all these factors and just try and reproduce this where you build it on the ARM host and the run it on the same ARM host and see how it goes. If it works there, then it's matter of slowly adding one component at a time and seeing at what point it starts failing.

> some more update, I have tried to run the local-cross build and generate bunch of binaries
> 
> ```
> -rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 26M Oct  2 03:15 skopeo.darwin.amd64
> -rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 23M Oct  2 03:15 skopeo.linux.arm
> -rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 25M Oct  2 03:16 skopeo.linux.arm64
> -rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 25M Oct  2 03:16 skopeo.windows.386.exe
> -rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 28M Oct  2 03:16 skopeo.windows.amd64.exe
> ```
> 
> however when I run the binary on the x86_64 machine (my mac) I can not run amd64 but able to run arm binary, is it suppose to be opposite?
> 
> ```
> root@14d9413fd9be:/go/src/github.com/containers/skopeo/bin# uname -m
> x86_64
> root@14d9413fd9be:/go/src/github.com/containers/skopeo/bin# ./skopeo.darwin.amd64 --version
> bash: ./skopeo.darwin.amd64: cannot execute binary file: Exec format error
> root@14d9413fd9be:/go/src/github.com/containers/skopeo/bin# ./skopeo.linux.arm --version
> skopeo version 1.4.1 commit: 130f32f047b7bf9b1fb4f95c4c5b1da6255e4829
> root@14d9413fd9be:/go/src/github.com/containers/skopeo/bin# ./skopeo.linux.arm64 --version
> skopeo version 1.4.1 commit: 130f32f047b7bf9b1fb4f95c4c5b1da6255e4829
> ```

how to build skopeo in arm64 ?
